contextMaps.title: The Three Context Maps
contextMaps.description: People operate from different internal maps depending on relational context. Maps load automatically through triggers and unresolved wounds. Regression is context-specific, NOT incompetence.

map1_work_adult.label: Map 1 — Work/Adult
map1_work_adult.description: Most current map. Competency-based with clear rules.
map1_work_adult.triggers: Performance reviews, deadlines, skill gaps, professional challenges

map2_social_adolescent.label: Map 2 — Social/Adolescent
map2_social_adolescent.description: Built in adolescence. Performance and masks.
map2_social_adolescent.triggers: Group dynamics, social media, comparison, peer judgment, wanting to fit in

map3_home_childhood.label: Map 3 — Home/Intimate/Childhood
map3_home_childhood.description: Oldest and deepest map. Belonging through BEING, not doing.
map3_home_childhood.triggers: Romantic partners, parents, authority figures reclassified as parental, abandonment fears, not being seen

contextMaps.key_insight: A boss can get reclassified as a parent figure, triggering the childhood map. Success in one map does NOT heal wounds in another.

emotionalCapacity.title: Suppression → Discharge → Capacity Spectrum
emotionalCapacity.description: The opposite of suppression is NOT expression — it's capacity.

detection_language: I understand why they did it
suppression.coaching_approach: Gently invite the user to feel what they are avoiding. Create safety first.

discharge.description: Spraying emotions out, making others carry your nervous system regulation
discharge.coaching_approach: Acknowledge the feeling, then redirect to ownership. Distinguish between sharing what's real vs. trying to move discomfort through someone else.

capacity.description: Holding the feeling, owning it, sharing responsibly without making it someone else's job
detection_language: I want to share something real
capacity.coaching_approach: Celebrate and reinforce. This is the goal state.

forceVsInfluence.title: Force vs. Influence Spectrum
forceVsInfluence.description: Whenever we use force (even subtle), we have to continue managing people instead of getting buy-in.

subtle_force.description: Overexplaining, hinting instead of asking, repeating 16 ways, adding urgency, charm, managing reactions, rescuing/doing it for them, withdrawal/withholding happiness
detection_language: I've explained it so many times
I try to make them understand
I do it for them because
subtle_force.coaching_approach: Name the subtle force pattern without judgment. Ask: 'What would clean influence look like here?'

clean_influence.description: Creating conditions where people WANT to follow — no management needed after buy-in

responsibility2.title: Responsibility 2.0
responsibility2.description: Not something you TAKE (heavy load) — it's a LENS for power. The moment you label something 'bad' you need a villain (self or other). Blame (self or other) = victim position.

key_distinctions.old_responsibility: Duty, blame, heaviness, 'should' — something you carry
key_distinctions.new_responsibility: A place to STAND and look out from, judgment-free — a lens for power
key_distinctions.preference_vs_judgment: 'I didn't want that' (preference/neutral) vs. 'that was bad' (judgment/needs villain)
key_distinctions.reframe: Many 'bad' things turned out to be necessary redirections

limitingBeliefs.title: Limiting Beliefs Framework (Byron Katie-inspired)
steps: Step 2: Could this be a misinterpretation or misperception?
Step 3: Do you have all possible information necessary?
Step 4: What do you feel/experience/become when you believe this negative thought?
Step 5: If this negative thought didn't exist, how would you feel/behave/experience life?

predictionErrors.title: Prediction Errors (Healing Mechanism)
predictionErrors.description: Deliberate vulnerable experiences that contradict old expectations. Help rewire nervous system maps toward safety.
predictionErrors.practice: Pause on a shift, take 2 breaths, name the map and age, stay there ~60 seconds without fixing

insightVsPractice.title: Insight vs. Practice
insightVsPractice.description: Insight alone does NOT create lasting change. Insight creates temporary relief/excitement, then regression to baseline. Practice = consistent repetition that builds new neural pathways.
insightVsPractice.quote: The difference between a believer and a non-believer: a believer takes action.
four_stages: Unconscious Unskilled: don't know what you don't know — acceptance is key
Conscious Unskilled: aware of gaps, danger zone where people quit
Conscious Skilled: adopting through repetition — 'repetition is the mother of all skills'
Unconscious Skilled: mastery, teaching others — this is where exponential results happen

boundaries.title: Boundaries
boundaries.description: A boundary is FOR YOU, not something you tell others to change their behavior. If it's a real boundary, there's no conversation, no justification, no explaining.
boundaries.key_insight: A boundary isn't something you stay after someone crosses it.
boundaries.analogy: Buy box: 'I only buy 2-bed 3-bath houses' — doesn't matter what comes across the table
boundaries.pattern: Over-functioning: making others comfortable at expense of self (often rooted in childhood caretaking)

victim.description: Blaming, shackled, attaching limiting meaning to experiences

healer.description: Taking chains off, choosing response, attaching empowering meaning

growthMetaphor.title: Gravitropic vs. Phototropic Growth
growthMetaphor.gravitropic: Growing roots DOWN — becoming grounded, stable, rooted in who you are
growthMetaphor.phototropic: Growing toward LIGHT — once roots are strong, you can withstand any storm
growthMetaphor.key_insight: Must build roots (inner work) before reaching for light (external achievement)

childhoodPatterns.title: Childhood Experience → Adult Patterns
childhoodPatterns.positive_responses: Staying present, tolerating anger, naming presence → trust, safety, boundary-setting, being seen without performing
childhoodPatterns.negative_responses: Punishment, dismissal, abandonment → fear, shame, people-pleasing, performance-based love, shutting down under criticism
childhoodPatterns.healing: Requires both: younger 'child' part that signals feeling + 'adult' part that holds with love and clarity

executionAccountability.title: Execution & Accountability
principles: Calendar is king: if it's not in your calendar, it's not happening
Comfort zone = unconscious protection. Growing feels uncomfortable — that's the signal you're on the right path.
We can't heal it until we feel it
Self-love, forgiveness, compassion, trust, consistency as foundational
Confidence built through working on self, not competition or proving
